title: ipomopsis aggregata subsp. weberi
intro: Ipomopsis aggregata subsp. weberi, also known as the sky pilot, is a flowering plant species native to the western United States and Canada. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of the sky pilot include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and pneumonia. Ipomopsis aggregata subsp. weberi is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture for medicinal use, although its effectiveness and safety as a treatment have not been extensively scientifically studied.
